'''Paul Hecht''' (born [[August 16]], [[1940s#1941|1941]]) is an [[Wikipedia:England|English]]-born [[Wikipedia:Canadians|Canadian]] stage, film, and television actor. Born in [[Wikipedia:London|London]], [[Wikipedia:England|England]], Hecht graduated from the [[Wikipedia:National Theatre School of Canada|National Theatre School of Canada]] in 1963.

He made his [[Wikipedia:Broadway theater|Broadway]] debut as "The Player" in ''[[Wikipedia:Rosencrantz & Guildenstern Are Dead|Rosencrantz & Guildenstern Are Dead]]'', for which he was nominated for the [[Wikipedia:Tony Award for Best Featured Actor in a Play|Tony Award for Best Performance by a Featured Actor in a Play]]. Hecht has numerous other Broadway, TV, and film credits to his name, and he has recorded extensively for the [[Wikipedia:Canadian Broadcasting Corporation|CBC]].<ref name="Bret Adams">{{WebCite|url=https://www.bretadamsltd.net/content/client/actors/paul-hecht/|text=Paul Hecht|work=Bret Adams, LTD - Artists' Agency|archivedate=20260110160307}}</ref>

In ''[[Star Wars]]'' media, Hecht performed the role of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] in the [[National Public Radio|NPR]] radio dramatizations of ''[[The Empire Strikes Back (radio)|The Empire Strikes Back]]'' and ''[[Return of the Jedi (radio)|Return of the Jedi]]''.
